product_updates.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
product_updates.content: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last wee…

product_updates.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
product_updates.content: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

product_updates.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
product_updates.content: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

product_updates.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
product_updates.content: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

product_updates.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
product_updates.content: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

product_updates.title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
product_updates.content: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” to improve its AI models.

product_updates.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
product_updates.content: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.

product_updates.title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
product_updates.content: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.

product_updates.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
product_updates.content: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping stone for promising AI startups.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI
technical_breakthroughs.content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers
technical_breakthroughs.content: Baek, a 35-year-old manager at the South Korean semiconductor titan SK Hynix, was enrolled in Sunoo, a matchmaking company based in Seoul, a year ago. In a move typical of anxious South Korean parents, his mother signed him up, hoping to find a good wife for her son. Lately, says Baek (who asked …
